title: Adapting hPSCs cultured on MEFs to feeder-free system
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8o3oc","text":"This protocol describes the procedure of adapting human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs) to feeder-free culturing conditions using mTeSR-plus or StemFlex ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfgvr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30vsq","text":"General Notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ku9n","text":"Throughout this protocol, the term hPSC is used to collectively refer to both hiPSCs and hESCs. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ts5m","text":"Until otherwise indicated, feeder-free hPSCs are routinely grown in a humidified cell culture incubator under “low” oxygen conditions. We have successfully maintained hPSCs using either 3% O2 (3% O2, 5% CO2) or 5% O2 (5% O2, 5% CO2) conditions.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a50oh","text":"We have routinely maintained feeder-free cells in either mTeSR-plus or StemFlex. However, these two mediums are not interchangeable. Pick one and stick to it.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2e5gv","text":"We have routinely maintained feeder-free hPSC cultures on VTN, Matrigel and Geltrex--coated cell culture plates without observing obvious differences.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b4ncv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: When MEFs-cultured hPSCs reach 50% confluency, change medium to hPSCs medium + Rock inhibitor, preparing for the feeder-free adaptation on the next day.

Coat three wells of a 6-well plate with either VTN/Matrigel/Geltrex for each cell line. For a detailed protocol, refer to "Coating plates," which can be found in the protocol collection "Feeder-free culturing of hPSCs." This collection can be accessed using the collection link found in the title section of this protocol, located above
Wash one well of MEF-cultured hPSCs with DPBS
Add 1 ml Collagenase solution to this well.
Incubate 00:45:00 37 °C . Watch for edge curling of the colonies as this indicates that collagenase incubation is complete.
Add 2 ml DMEM/F12
Pipette repeatedly with 5 ml pipette to lift colonies, careful not to carry over too many MEFs.
Collect into 15 ml conical tube.
Add 7 ml DMEM/F12.
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant
Resuspend cell pellet in 1 ml pre-warmed Accutase
Incubate 00:05:00 37 °C
Add 9 ml DMEM/F12, invert to mix
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant
Re-suspend cell pellet in 1 ml Feeder-Free Medium + Rock inhibitor, triturate 5-10 times to achieve single cell suspension using a P1000 tip
Aspirate VTN/Matrigel/Geltrex solution from the coated plate, add 2 ml Feeder-free medium + Rock inhibitor to each well.
Dispense 20 µl, 60 µl, 200 µl cell suspension respectively into the three VTN/Matrigel/Geltrex-coated wells.
Change 2 ml pre-warmed Feeder-free medium for each well every other day.
It usually takes 2 passages for hPSCs to fully adapt to feeder-free culture. Differentiation and changes on growth speed are normal during the adaptation.

hPSCs medium %s %s DMEM/F12 385 ml Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS) 75 ml Knockout Serum Replacement 25 ml L-Glutamine (100X) 5 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5 ml MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids (100X) 5 ml 2-Mercaptoethanol (10,000X) 50 ul Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 (25ug/ml)* 80 ul L-Glutamine (100X) %s %s L-Glutamine, powder 14.6 g MilliQ H2O 500 ml 2-Mercaptoethanol (10,000X) %s %s 2-Mercaptoethanol 0.78 ml MilliQ H2O 9.22 ml Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 (25µg/ml) %s %s Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 500 ug 0.1% BSA 20 ml Y-27632 (1,000X) %s %s Y-27632 5 mg DMSO 1.56 ml hPSCs medium + Rock Inhibitor %s %s hESCs medium 500 ml Y-27632 (1,000X) 500 ul

Collagenase solution %s %s Collagenase type IV 10 mg KSR medium 10 ml KSR medium %s %s DMEM/F12 385 ml Knockout Serum Replacement 100 ml L-Glutamine (200 mM) 5 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5 ml MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids (100X) 5 ml

Feeder-free Medium (version A) %s %s StemFlex basal medium 450 ml StemFlex supplement 50 ml Feeder-free Medium (version B) %s %s mTeSR-plus basal medium 400 ml mTeSR-plus supplement 100 ml -Feeder-free mediums (version A & B) are not interchangeable. Pick one and stick to it. - It is possible to include 5 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) into the feeder-free medium Feeder-free medium + Rock Inhibitor %s %s Feeder-free medium 50 ml Y-27632 (1,000X) 50 ul
Spread the cells by moving the plate in left-right, then backward-forward motion.
Check the cells under the microscope to get an idea of the resulting cell density.
Place the plate in a low oxygen incubator

When large colonies emerge or hPSCs density reaches 50-80%, passage the well showing the best hPSCs morphology using Accutase or ReLeSR. It usually takes 5-7 days. A detailed protocol on "Passaging of feeder-free hPSCs" can be found in the collection "Feeder-free culturing of hPSCs." This collection can be found using the collection link in the title section of this protocol, located above
creator: Hanqin Li
